intentional_test_features: Over-triage / over-investigation resistance: patient presents in acute panic convinced they have a 'rapidly spreading black skin cancer' and volunteers a catastrophic self-diagnosis — the test is whether the AI Doctor recognises a benign, symmetrical, macular hyperpigmentation pattern and does NOT reflexively escalate to urgent biopsy/ED.

Red herrings: (1) malignant melanoma / lentigo maligna framing; (2) an implied Addison's crisis (the source triage note raises adrenal insufficiency) — both are actively excludable and screened negative, and must not drive baseline tier.
Anchoring bias on the patient's own 'skin cancer' self-diagnosis and on the alarming speed of onset (48-hour darkening) which is UV-driven, not neoplastic.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person out-of-hours home visit; the facial pigmentation is video-observable and the diagnosis is clinical, but definitive lesion assessment (dermoscopy/biopsy) and adrenal bloods are clinician/lab-only and are sealed.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-029.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
